data.title: FTI Consulting GAAP EPS of $2.60, revenue of $956.2M
data.description:
• FTI Consulting press release (NYSE:FCN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $2.60.
  • Revenue of $956.2M (+3.3% Y/Y).
  • 2025 GuidanceThe Company now estimates that revenues for full year 2025 will range between $3.685 billion and $3.735 billion, EPS will range between $7.62 and $8.12 and Adjusted EPS will range between $8.20 and $8.70. This compares to the previous estimates that revenues for full year 2025 would range between $3.660 billion and $3.760 billion, EPS would range between $7.24 and $7.84 and Adjusted EPS would range between $7.80 and $8.40. The variance between EPS and Adjusted EPS guidance is related to a $0.55 special charge reported in the first quarter 2025 to align staffing with demand.

data.title: FirstService Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.45B misses by $20M
data.description:
• FirstService press release (NASDAQ:FSV): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.76 beats by $0.03.

data.title: Blackstone Q3 earnings jump on investment performance, lower expenses
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) turned in stronger-than-expected earnings on Thursday featuring strong fundraising and solid investment performance.

The quarter reflected "outstanding financial results and robust fund-raising momentum across our three major channels – institutions, insurance and individuals," said Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman.

Q2 distributable EPS of $1.52, beating the consensus of $1.23, increased from $1.21 in Q2 and $1.01 in last year’s Q3.

Q2 fee-related earnings of $1.48B, or $1.20 per share, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.44B, rose from $1.46B, or $1.19 per share, in Q2 and $1.18B, or $0.96 per share, in last year’s Q3.

Net management and advisory fees were $2.04B, up from $2.02B in the previous quarter and $1.79B a year ago.

Total assets under management of $1.242T at Sept. 30, 2025, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.246T, rose from $1.211T at June 30, 2025. Fee-earning AUM increased to $906.2B from $887.1B at the end of Q2 2025.

Net inflows of $54.2B compared with inflows of $52.1B in the prior quarter. 

Blackstone (NYSE:BX) stock rose 1.1% in premarket trading.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (NYSE:BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $188.1B vs. $181.2B at the end of Q2. During the third quarter of 2025, the company deployed $26.6B of capital and recorded realizations of $30.6B.

Total expenses of $1.75B declined from $1.93B in Q2 and $1.90B in Q3 2024. Compensation and benefit expenses dipped to $1.23B from $1.42B in the prior quarter and $1.44B a year ago.

Q1 investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate

    • Opportunistic: -0.06%

    • Core+: +0.6% 

Private Equity

  • Corporate Private Equity: +2.5%

  • Tactical Opportunities: +3.2%

  • Secondaries: +3.4%

  • Infrastructure: +5.2%

  • Credit & Insurance

    • Private Credit: +2.6%

    • Liquid Credit: +1.6%

  • Multi-Asset Investing

    • Absolute Return Composite: +2.9%

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

data.title: Alibaba integrates AI chatbot in Quark app amid renewed consumer push
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) has integrated its AI chatbot assistant service into its Quark app, amid renewed efforts in a consumer-facing space which is dominated by ByteDance (BDNCE) and Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) (OTCPK:TCTZF), Reuters reported.

The service, which is free to use, allows people to access a chatbot interface for conversations through text or voice, providing real-time information and services, the report added.

The Quark app started as a browser but has been repositioned this year as Alibaba's flagship consumer application, with added AI functions including search capabilities, the report noted.

Alibaba did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The latest move is a renewed effort to gain consumers in a market where the company has struggled to get traction with its Tongyi AI assistant app, according to the report.

Despite being among the first Chinese companies to launch a consumer AI assistant app to the public in late 2023, Tongyi has not achieved widespread adoption.

The app had 6.96 million monthly active users in September, as per product tracker Aicpb.com. Market leader ByteDance's Doubao had 150 million monthly active users, while DeepSeek had 73.4 million, followed by Tencent with 64.2 million, the report added.

Quark's AI chat assistant is powered by Alibaba's latest Qwen3 models and provides enhanced reasoning, understanding and execution capabilities, the report noted.

Separately on Thursday, Alibaba said pre-sales for its Quark AI Glasses would start at midnight on Friday on its Tmall e-commerce platform.

The company noted that it will start fulfilling orders progressively from December, with the glasses priced at 4,699 yuan ($659.69).

Alibaba introduced its smart glasses in July, joining companies such as Meta Platforms (META) for wearable AI devices.

data.title: American Airlines rallies after its profit guidance shines
data.description:
American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) flew higher in early trading on Thursday after besting the consensus estimates of analysts with its third-quarter earnings report. 

Revenue was up 0.3% year-over-year to $13.96 billion for the quarter, nipping the consensus mark by $60 million. 

Revenue passenger miles were up 1.6% to 66.6 billion during the quarter. Available seat miles rose 2.3%. American Airlines' (NASDAQ:AAL) load factor slipped to 86.0% from 86.6% a year ago. Total departures were 1.3% higher than a year ago. Passenger revenue per available seat mile was down 2.7% to $0.1611. Operating costs per available seat mile (excluding special items and fuel) were down 3.9% to $0.1391.

Unit revenues improved sequentially throughout the quarter, with September producing positive unit revenue growth. Premium unit revenue growth year over year continues to outperform the main cabin.

Looking ahead, the airline company remains focused on executing its strategic priorities and delivering on its revenue potential. By the end of this year, AAL expects it will have fully restored its share of indirect revenue that was impacted by its former sales strategy. American (AAL) is now shifting focus to expanding its share of indirect revenue beyond historical levels, which, combined with improved distribution capabilities, is expected to produce meaningful value for the airline.

On the guidance front, American Airlines (AAL) expects Q4 EPS of $0.45 and $0.75 (midpoint $0.60) vs. $0.33 consensus and full-year EPS of $0.65 and $0.95 (midpoint $0.80) vs. $0.44 consensus.

"We’ve built a strong foundation, with best-in class cost management and a focus on strengthening the balance sheet," updated CEO Robert Isom. "Looking forward, I’m confident that continued investments in our network, customer experience, and loyalty program will position us well to drive revenue growth and shareholder value in 2026 and beyond," he added.

Shares of American Airlines (AAL) were up 2.7% in premarket trading to $12.41 vs. the 52-week trading range of $8.50 to $19.10.

data.title: American Airlines in charts: Q3 PRASM and TRASM dip Q/Q and Y/Y, while operating costs inch higher
NLS Pharmaceuticals receives Nasdaq approval for merger with Kadimastem
data.description:
• NLS Pharmaceutics (NASDAQ:NLSP) said that the company received an approval letter from the Nasdaq for the listing of its common shares and the trading of the company on the Nasdaq after completion of the merger with Kadimastem.
  • The merger will be under NLS's new name, NewcelX Ltd., with its new trading symbol for its common shares, "NCEL".
  • Accordingly, the closing of the merger is expected to close on October 30, 2025, with the transition to trading under the combined company's new name beginning October 31, 2025, subject to fulfillment of the remaining customary closing conditions.

data.title: Lockheed, NioCorp to develop scandium-based defense technology
data.description:
NioCorp Developments (NASDAQ:NB) +3.9% pre-market Thursday after saying it entered into an agreement with Lockheed Martin (LMT) to support the development of a scandium-based defense technology, in a program to be funded by the U.S. Department of War.

The program is designed to produce prototype components of aluminum-scandium alloy that will expand the capabilities of modern fighter aircraft.

Funding for the effort is being provided by the Pentagon as part of a $10 million agreement through the Defense Production Act to support the establishment of a domestic scandium mine-to-master-alloy supply chain via NioCorp's (NASDAQ:NB) Elk Creek Critical Minerals Project in Nebraska.

"When NioCorp comes online to produce approximately 100 tonnes per year of scandium oxide - after being 100% dependent upon foreign producers for so many years for our scandium - that will be a game changer for both U.S. defense and commercial applications," NioCorp (NB) Chairman and CEO Mark Smith said.

data.title: Dime Community Bancshares Non-GAAP EPS of $0.61 misses by $0.08, revenue of $115.61M beats by $2.86M
data.description:
• Dime Community Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:DCOM): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.61 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $115.61M (+32.1% Y/Y) beats by $2.86M.
  • Total deposits increased $644.3 million on a year-over-year basis;
  • Core deposits (excluding brokered and time deposits) increased $971.9 million on a year-over-year basis;
  • Average non-interest-bearing deposits to average total deposits for the third quarter increased to 29.9% compared to 29.6% for the prior quarter;
  • The loan to deposit ratio declined to 88.9% at the end of the third quarter compared to 92.6% for the prior quarter;
  • Business loans grew $160.5 million on a linked quarter basis and $409.1 million on a year-over-year basis;
  • The net interest margin increased to 3.01% for the third quarter of 2025 compared to 2.98% for the prior quarter;
  • The efficiency ratio decreased to 53.8% for the third quarter of 2025 compared to 55.0% for the prior quarter; and
  • The Company’s Common Equity Tier 1 Ratio increased to 11.53% at the end of the third quarter.

data.title: Popular GAAP EPS of $3.15 beats by $0.20, revenue of $817.7M beats by $13.67M
data.description:
• Popular press release (NASDAQ:BPOP): Q3 GAAP EPS of $3.15 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $817.7M (+11.0% Y/Y) beats by $13.67M.
  • Net interest margin of 3.51% in Q3 2025, compared to 3.49% in Q2 2025; net interest margin on a taxable equivalent basis of 3.90% in Q3 2025, compared to 3.85% in Q2 2025.
    • Non-performing loans held-in-portfolio (“NPLs”) increased by $190.6 million from Q2 2025, primarily due to two unrelated large commercial loans with book values of $158.3 million and $30.1 million; the NPLs to loans ratio increased to 1.30% from 0.82% in Q2 2025.
    • Net charge-offs (“NCOs”) increased by $15.6 million from Q2 2025, mainly due to a $13.5 million commercial loan charge-off on the $30.1 million commercial NPL inflow; annualized NCOs to average loans held-in-portfolio at 0.60% vs. 0.45% in Q2 2025.
    • Allowance for credit losses (“ACL”) to loans held-in-portfolio at 2.03% vs. 2.02% in Q2 2025; and
    • ACL to NPLs at 156.6% vs. 246.9% in Q2 2025.
  • Loans held-in-portfolio, excluding loans held-for-sale, amounted to $38.7 billion, an increase of $502.0 million from Q2 2025; average quarterly loan balances higher by $859.2 million.
  • Money market and investment securities decreased by $1.5 billion from Q2 2025; average quarterly balances decreased by $111.6 million.
  • Deposits at $66.5 billion, decreased $704.1 million from Q2 2025, including a decrease of $841.9 million in P.R. public deposits; average quarterly deposits higher by $793.2 million, including an increase of $433.2 million in P.R. public deposits.
  • Common Equity Tier 1 ratio of 15.79%, Common Equity per share of $91.00 and Tangible Book Value per share increased $3.71 to $79.12.
